metrics.narrative: PIF's stated driver is 'automating manual processes and deploying AI automation' (MEDDPICC field, updated 6 Apr 2026), corroborated by the 8 Oct 2025 on-site note that PIF will run a comprehensive, whole-of-market S2P exercise. No buyer-quoted quantified value (cost, cycle-time, headcount) has been captured in any source; the business case remains directional, not measured.

champion.narrative: No confirmed champion. The CRM's own Champion field (updated 6 Apr 2026) is candid that PIF stakeholders 'will be a very formal process... suspect they will be ultra-professional - at least well into the late RFP stage.' Andrew Waterhouse is the primary contact, but his most recent message (confirmed via the 2 Jul 2026 follow-up) — that 'nothing required from Zycus team at this stage' — reads as neutral gatekeeping, not advocacy. No evidence anywhere of a PIF-side individual actively selling Zycus internally.

competition.narrative: The competitive field is explicitly and fully named: SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua and JAGGAER (Competitors__c), with MEDDPICC noting Zycus expects to face 'at least all other MQ Leaders' and intends to differentiate on AI/Agentic maturity. However, this is a structural list, not live comparative intelligence — Efficio, the process gatekeeper, explicitly declined on 20 Mar 2026 to be influenced or to share standing ('not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic'), so Zycus has no visibility into how it is actually scoring against these rivals.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is named generically ('automating manual processes and deploying AI automation') but not detailed to specific processes, systems, or cost of the status quo. The 8 Oct 2025 meeting confirmed the exercise will cover the 'whole S2P market' for PIF itself, suggesting a broad modernization mandate rather than a narrowly scoped pain point.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics exist yet, appropriate for a deal still awaiting formal RFP issuance. The one structural paper-adjacent risk on record is the CRM Blockers field: 'local hosting, legal entity/office, regional HQ... unless we partner with EY et al' — Zycus may not currently satisfy PIF's expected local-presence requirement, which could gate any eventual award independent of RFP scoring.

economic_buyer.narrative: Abdulaziz Alsuwailem is explicitly named SVP / Economic Buyer in the current Next_Step__c text, so the role is structurally identified. However, he has had zero direct Zycus engagement to date; the account owner's first attempt to reach him only began 18 Jun 2026, via a drafted introduction shared internally and routed through the partner channel. All engagement so far has run through Andrew Waterhouse (PIF project lead) and Efficio, not the EB himself.

decision_process.narrative: The process is well documented and is being followed step by step: registration/NDA/code-of-conduct requested 12 Jan 2026 and resolved after registration issues 7 Feb 2026; RFI received 2 Mar 2026 (ahead of schedule) and submitted 15 Mar 2026 ahead of the 15 Mar deadline; the process is now sitting between RFI and formal RFP issuance, confirmed still pending as of 2 Jul 2026. The staged sequence (registration -> RFI -> RFP -> demo workshops -> clarifications -> shortlist -> negotiation -> decision) matches the CRM's Decision Process field exactly.

decision_criteria.narrative: MEDDPICC records the criteria only at the process level: 'Formal RFP & negotiation process between all the top tier providers.' The 8 Oct 2025 on-site meeting confirmed PIF will run a formal, whole-of-S2P-market exercise covering its own entity (not portfolio companies), but no scored criteria, weightings, or BRD have been shared with Zycus.

items.name: Andy shuts down Brien's follow-up, says no review needed now
items.summary: Responding to Brien's request for a 30-min working session on PIF's S2C initiative, Andy Waterhouse told him to stop copying departed PIF staff and route all communication through Carl, Dan and Hakim to him, adding that PIF does not need further review of the Zycus offering at this time — a clear stall signal on the deal.

items.name: Carl apologizes and confirms single-channel comms
items.summary: Carl Kimball quickly responded to Andy's rebuke, apologizing and confirming that all communications will now go through Hakim and himself, and that the internal team has been updated — an attempt to repair the relationship and unblock further engagement.

items.name: Andy acknowledges Carl's correction
items.summary: Andy sent a brief one-line reply thanking Carl for confirming the consolidated communication channel, closing the loop on the process issue for now.

items.name: Andy resurfaces after holiday, asks for single channel and KAFD visit
items.summary: In an earlier reply on the same thread, Andy explained he'd been on holiday, noted he was also getting separate emails from Brien Rodrigues and asked Carl to consolidate to one channel, while inviting Carl to propose dates to meet in person at KAFD — the root cause of the day's channel-cleanup exchange.

day_summary.overall: A messy multi-thread email exchange forced a hard reset on how Zycus engages PIF: Andy Waterhouse pushed back on Brien's outreach, pointing out several cc'd contacts have left PIF and stating there's no need for further review of the Zycus offering right now, while insisting all communication be consolidated through Carl, Dan and Hakim to him directly. Carl quickly conceded the point and confirmed the team will now route everything through Hakim and himself, and separately worked to set up an in-person meeting with Andy in KAFD. Net effect: the deal's active review is paused/stalled per the buyer, and the priority has shifted from pitching capabilities to first fixing the channel and re-earning a working session.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A long-running, partner-led Saudi opportunity that has cleared its early process gates (registration, RFI) cleanly but has stalled on the buyer's own timeline waiting for the formal RFP, with no direct economic-buyer access and no confirmed champion after nine-plus months of engagement.

deal_risk.text: Close date of 31 Jul 2026 is not achievable — with the formal RFP still unissued as of 2 Jul 2026, the remaining 22 days cannot fit RFP issuance, demos, clarifications, shortlisting and negotiation.

KSA local hosting / legal entity gap — per the CRM Blockers field (updated 6 Apr 2026), the exercise is expected to require local hosting and a regional legal entity/office that Zycus does not currently have, absent a partner like EY.

Single-threaded relationship — the only substantive buyer-side relationship is with Andrew Waterhouse; Abdullah Almanyah, Lujain Mosaeb, and the generic Supplier Relationship Management mailbox are on record as contacts but with no logged direct engagement this window.

win_position.text: Economic buyer never engaged — Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP) has had zero direct Zycus contact; the first attempt to reach him began only 18 Jun 2026 via an internal BD introduction, per Next_Step_History__c.
Formal RFP still not issued — anticipated 'after Eid' (end March 2026) per the 7 Feb 2026 note, Andrew Waterhouse confirmed on 13 May 2026 there was still no update, pushing the process 'back to Q2'.
Registration and RFI milestones cleared cleanly — supplier registration/NDA closed 7 Feb 2026 and the RFI was submitted 15 Mar 2026 ahead of the 15 Mar deadline.

No champion secured — the CRM's own Champion assessment (updated 6 Apr 2026) predicts PIF stakeholders will stay 'ultra-professional' well into the late RFP stage, and Andrew Waterhouse's 2 Jul 2026 message that 'nothing required from Zycus' reads as gatekeeping, not advocacy.
Long, continuous relationship since 10 Sep 2025 — an in-person meeting at PIF Tower on 8 Oct 2025 and repeated event invitations have kept Zycus present through the slow RFP runway.

deal_momentum.text: Deal is idle on the buyer side — the only movement since the 15 Mar 2026 RFI submission has been rep-initiated follow-ups (13 May, 18 Jun, 2 Jul 2026); the one buyer reply (around 22 Jun 2026) simply said nothing further is needed right now.
A genuine two-way touch landed 17 days ago — Andrew Waterhouse's reply around 22 Jun 2026 confirms the relationship is live, even without a forward ask.

Efficio has closed the influence channel — on 20 Mar 2026 Efficio told Zycus's alliances team directly 'not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic', removing any lever to accelerate or shape the timeline.

customer_commitment.text: No forward buyer commitment logged — every dated Next_Step_History__c entry since Oct 2025 is a registration step, an RFI/RFP status check, or an event invite; no buyer-initiated ask exists on record.
PIF completed its own process steps on schedule — NDA/code-of-conduct cleared 7 Feb 2026 and the RFI accepted 15 Mar 2026, showing the buyer's process is genuinely active, not abandoned.
